proposition_label: Scripture has a spiritual and allegorical sense beyond the literal
description: Scripture contains multiple layers of meaning — literal, moral, and spiritual/allegorical — and the deeper spiritual sense is often the most important for the soul's progress toward God.

proposition_label: Almsgiving is Required
description: Christians are obligated to give alms to the poor as stewards of God's gifts; wealth deployed through giving redeems sins and fulfills the commandment of love.

proposition_label: Faith is transmitted through apostolic succession
description: The bishops who succeed the apostles are authoritative guarantors of the received tradition.

proposition_label: The ascetic covenant (bnay qyama) is the normative Christian calling
description: Baptized Christians are called to a consecrated ascetic life as members of a covenant community (bnay qyama / bnat qyama); this is the normative rather than exceptional form of Christian discipleship in early Syriac tradition.

proposition_label: Baptism is administered in the Trinitarian name of Father, Son, and Holy Spirit
description: Baptism uses the explicit Trinitarian formula of Father, Son, and Holy Spirit as the form of the sacrament.

proposition_label: Baptism effects spiritual regeneration
description: Baptism is not merely symbolic but effects a new birth or regeneration of the recipient.

proposition_label: Baptism is called illumination, by which the soul receives the light of salvation
description: Baptism is not merely a washing but an illumination of the soul — the reception of divine light, adoption as children of God, and entry into immortal life.

proposition_label: Baptism is administered with water
description: Baptismal practice uses water as the material element.

proposition_label: Baptismal Anointing with Oil
description: After emerging from the baptismal water, the newly baptized is anointed with chrism or oil as part of the baptismal rite, signifying the reception of the Holy Spirit and incorporation into Christ.

proposition_label: The apostle Bartholomew preached in India and left a Hebrew Gospel of Matthew
description: Eusebius HE 5.10 reports that Pantaenus found in India Christians who possessed a Hebrew Matthew left by the apostle Bartholomew; Philostorgius HE 3.6 also mentions Bartholomew.

proposition_label: Valid Eucharist requires the bishop or his delegate
description: Proper eucharistic celebration is administered by or authorized by the bishop.

proposition_label: The succession of bishops in their see guarantees the apostolic faith
description: The see of the bishop, transmitted through orderly succession, is the locus and guarantee of the faith received from the apostles.

proposition_label: A single bishop governs the local church
description: A single bishop holds authority over the local Christian community as representative of God.

proposition_label: The human body will be raised from the dead at the last day
description: At the final judgment God will raise the same bodies that died, reuniting them with their souls for eternal life or punishment.

proposition_label: A formal catechumenate precedes baptism
description: Candidates for baptism undergo a structured period of pre-baptismal instruction, examination, and moral formation (the catechumenate) before being admitted to the sacrament.

proposition_label: Christ's dominion extends to all nations beyond the Roman frontier
description: Tertullian Adv. Jud. 7 rhetorically lists all nations reached by Christ including those beyond Roman access; an early theological claim of universal Christian presence.

proposition_label: Christ truly became flesh and truly suffered in the body
description: The incarnate Christ was a genuine human being who truly was born, truly suffered, truly died, and truly rose again; not a phantom or appearance as the Docetists claimed.

proposition_label: Christ Truly Suffered and Died
description: Christ's passion and death on the cross were genuine physical events, not apparent, symbolic, or borne by a substitute. Denied by docetists, Basilides (Simon Cyrene substitution), Marcion, and Cerinthus.

proposition_label: Christian communities were established in the Arabian Peninsula
description: Christianity was established in the Arabian Peninsula including at Bostra (synod c. 244), and in Himyar through Theophilus the Indian's mission c. 354 (Philostorgius); Najran attested later.

proposition_label: Christianity was established in Ethiopia through Frumentius and the conversion of King Ezana
description: The Aksumite kingdom (Ethiopia) received Christianity through the mission of Frumentius, consecrated by Athanasius of Alexandria, who converted King Ezana c. 330; attested by Rufinus, Socrates, Sozomen.

proposition_label: Christianity reached Britannia before the end of the Roman period
description: Christianity was present in Roman Britain; rhetorical attestation by Tertullian c. 200, Origen c. 244, and firm documentary evidence of 3 British bishops at the Council of Arles 314.

proposition_label: Christians are instructed to abstain from public games, theaters, and spectacles
description: Public games, theaters, and spectacles are morally dangerous entertainments that Christians must avoid in favor of the word of God.

proposition_label: Christians fast on Wednesday and Friday
description: The Didache mandates fasting on Wednesday and Friday as distinctively Christian fast days, distinguishing Christians from the Jewish practice of fasting on Monday and Thursday.

proposition_label: Christians must not serve in the military
description: Military service is incompatible with Christian discipleship due to the requirements of idolatrous oath-taking, bloodshed, and submission to pagan authority.

proposition_label: Christians Must Observe Mosaic Law
description: Christian salvation requires observance of the Mosaic law including circumcision. Held by Ebionites and Judaizers; opposed by Paul, the Jerusalem Council, and all mainstream post-Pauline tradition.

proposition_label: Disciples are called to follow Jesus
description: Jesus summons persons into direct discipleship.

proposition_label: Radical continence is required of all Christians
description: Sexual continence, abstinence from meat, and/or abstinence from wine are required — not merely counseled — for all Christians; associated with Tatian, the Acts of Thomas, and some early Syriac communities.

proposition_label: The unity of bishops grounds the unity of the Church
description: The Church is one because the episcopate is one; each bishop holds the whole of the one episcopate in solidum; to break with the bishop is to break with the Church; there is one altar and one bishop.

proposition_label: The Eucharist fulfills Malachi's prophecy of the pure offering in every place
description: The Christian Eucharist is identified as the fulfillment of Malachi 1:11 — the pure sacrifice offered to God in every place throughout the nations.

proposition_label: The Eucharist is the body and blood of Christ
description: The eucharistic elements are identified as the flesh and blood of Jesus.

proposition_label: The Eucharist is medicine of immortality
description: The eucharistic bread confers immortality and incorruption on those who receive it; sharing in Christ's deathless life.

proposition_label: The Eucharist is a sacrifice offered to God
description: The eucharistic celebration is explicitly described as a sacrifice or oblation offered to God.

proposition_label: Christians gather on Sunday for the Eucharist
description: The Lord's Day is the regular day for eucharistic assembly.

proposition_label: Christians are required to fast
description: Fasting is a required Christian spiritual discipline presented as obligatory, not merely optional.

proposition_label: A final judgment is coming
description: Texts or teachers present a climactic future judgment of the wicked and vindication of the righteous.

proposition_label: Human free will is the basis for moral responsibility before God
description: God created rational beings with genuine freedom of choice; this freedom is what makes praise, blame, reward, and punishment just; evil arises from the misuse of free will, not from God or from nature.

proposition_label: Christians should assemble frequently for Eucharist
description: Regular gathering for eucharistic worship is normative.

proposition_label: Gentile believers need not be circumcised
description: Gentile inclusion in the people of God does not require circumcision.

proposition_label: A harmonized gospel narrative is the authoritative gospel text
description: The Diatessaron or similar gospel harmony serves as the primary authoritative gospel text for liturgy and teaching, rather than reading four separate canonical gospels; standard in the Syriac church from c. 170 until the 5th century.

proposition_label: The gospel reached India through early missionary or apostolic activity
description: Christianity reached India — whether the Malabar coast or the broader "India" of ancient usage — through apostolic (Thomas, Bartholomew) or early missionary (Pantaenus) activity; attested by Eusebius HE 5.10 and Philostorgius.

proposition_label: Greek philosophy served as a preparatory schoolmaster leading Gentiles toward the Gospel
description: Before the advent of Christ, philosophy served for the Greeks as what the Law served for the Hebrews — a pedagogue preparing them for the righteousness and faith found fully in Christ.

proposition_label: Baptism administered by heretics or schismatics is invalid and must be repeated
description: Baptism conferred outside the one Church by heretics or schismatics is no baptism at all; converts from heretical groups must be baptized anew because the Holy Spirit operates only within the Church. Held by Cyprian and the African councils; opposed by Stephen of Rome who accepted heretical baptism if in trinitarian form.

proposition_label: Infants and children receive baptism
description: Infant and child baptism is explicitly affirmed or presupposed as the ordinary practice of the Church.

proposition_label: Jesus was truly born of a virgin
description: Jesus was truly conceived by the Holy Spirit and born of the virgin Mary, not through natural human generation.

proposition_label: Jesus is proclaimed as raised
description: The apostolic proclamation announces that Jesus has been raised from the dead.

proposition_label: Jesus is the Christ
description: Texts or teachers explicitly identify Jesus as the Christ or Messiah.

proposition_label: Jesus teaches with unique authority
description: Jesus is portrayed as speaking with authority that stands out from other teachers.

proposition_label: The kingdom of God has drawn near
description: Jesus proclamation presents God kingdom as imminently present or arriving.

proposition_label: Christ the Logos is the pre-existent Son of God
description: The Word/Logos of God, who became incarnate as Jesus Christ, pre-existed creation as the Son and mind of the Father, through whom all things were made.

proposition_label: Martyrdom is bearing faithful witness to Christ before authorities
description: The martyr's death is presented as a form of witness to Christ, conforming to his passion and declaring undivided fidelity before earthly powers.

proposition_label: The bones of martyrs are precious and gathered for commemoration
description: The bodily remains of martyrs are treated as precious, gathered for custody, and commemorated at annual anniversary gatherings.

proposition_label: Old Testament is Authoritative Scripture
description: The Church accepts the Old Testament as authoritative Christian scripture; the God of creation and Israel is the same God as the Father of Jesus Christ. Denied by Marcion, Valentinus, Basilides, and most Gnostic schools.

proposition_label: Ordination is conferred by the laying on of hands
description: The ministry of bishop, presbyter, and deacon is conferred through the liturgical act of laying on of hands by existing bishops (for episcopal ordination) or by the bishop (for presbyters and deacons).

proposition_label: Mary remained ever-virgin — before, during, and after the birth of Jesus
description: The mother of Jesus was a virgin not only in conceiving him by the Holy Spirit but remained perpetually a virgin; the 'brothers' of Jesus are not biological children of Mary.

proposition_label: Post-baptismal sin can be forgiven through penance
description: A second repentance after baptism is possible and administered through church discipline.

proposition_label: Prayer is offered without ceasing throughout all of life
description: The spiritually formed Christian does not confine prayer to set times but prays continually through every word, action, and disposition, maintaining unceasing communion with God.

proposition_label: Human souls pre-exist before their embodiment
description: Before the creation of the material world, God created rational souls who existed in a prior spiritual state; their embodiment is a consequence of their varying responses to God in that prior state. Taught by Origen; later condemned at the Fifth Ecumenical Council (553).

proposition_label: Prophetic Gifts Continue After the Apostles
description: The Holy Spirit continues to give direct prophecy and ecstatic spiritual gifts beyond the apostolic age; new prophetic revelation may supplement or correct the established tradition. Affirmed by Montanists; opposed by the emerging episcopal mainstream who grounded authority in apostolic succession.

proposition_label: Repentance is required in response to the proclamation
description: The proclamation of the kingdom calls hearers to repentance.

proposition_label: The rule of faith is received from the apostles
description: Christians hold to a received standard of teaching traced to the apostles and guarded by their successors.

proposition_label: Second marriage after the death of a spouse is forbidden
description: Remarriage after the death of a spouse constitutes a species of adultery; Christians must practice strict monogamy, marrying only once.

proposition_label: The Son is eternally begotten from the Father
description: The Son/Logos is not a creature brought into being at a point in time but is eternally generated from the substance of the Father — there was never a time when the Son was not. Key Origenian contribution that became foundational for Nicene orthodoxy.

proposition_label: The apostle Thomas preached in India
description: The Acts of Thomas (3rd c.) narrates Thomas's journey to India; the Thomas Christian tradition in Malabar claims apostolic origin; certainty is claimed_tradition.

proposition_label: Valid church order requires bishop, presbyter, and deacon
description: The threefold ministry of bishop, presbyter, and deacon is required for valid church structure.

proposition_label: God is three persons — Father, Son, and Holy Spirit — in one substance
description: The one God subsists in three persons (personae) — Father, Son, and Holy Spirit — who share one substance (substantia); the Son proceeds from the Father and the Spirit from both, without dividing the divine substance.

proposition_label: All rational beings will eventually be restored to God (apokatastasis)
description: At the end of all ages, every rational creature — including the damned and possibly demons — will be purified and restored to communion with God; punishment is remedial, not eternal. Taught by Origen in De Principiis; later condemned but influential in Eastern theology.

proposition_label: Virginity and celibacy are honored as a positive Christian calling
description: Virginity and celibacy are not merely tolerated but positively honored as a higher calling, even while marriage is defended as legitimate and good.
